title: link: implement encrypted frame payload handling
description: Feature-completeness gap found during Zephyr firmware status audit. Link-layer RX explicitly rejects encrypted frames because link-layer encryption is not implemented yet. The protocol architecture calls for link security in addition to OSCORE, so firmware is not security feature-complete. Evidence: lichen/subsys/lichen/link/lichen_link_rx.c:183 states link-layer encryption is not implemented and encrypted frames are not passed to SCHC; TX comments also describe MIC fallback behavior but not encryption. Acceptance: implement link-layer encryption/decryption for the agreed frame format using vetted crypto, keep OSCORE ordering intact, reject unauthenticated/tampered encrypted frames, add test vectors or unit tests for encrypted RX/TX paths, and ensure insecure CRC32 fallback remains opt-in and clearly non-production.
acceptance_criteria: After implementation, run the relevant tests/builds and then complete two separate code-review passes before closing. Each review pass should inspect the produced code against this Bead's acceptance criteria and file follow-up Beads for any discovered issues.
close_reason: Implemented AES-CCM encrypted link-frame TX/RX handling, added encrypted link crypto tests, ran quality gates, completed two review passes, and filed follow-up project-LICHEN-9nrr.17 for AES-CCM module coupling.
